answer: The Tokyo metropolitan area had an estimated population of 37.4 million in 2024. This makes it the largest urban region globally. The UN estimates the population at 36.95 million as of 2026.

results.title: Tokyo's metropolitan area is the largest urban region in the world ...
results.snippet: # The World in Maps’ Post. Tokyo’s metropolitan area is the largest urban region in the world, with an estimated ~37.4 million inhabitants in 2024. What makes this striking is the comparison with many countries shown in grey on the map: places that are geographically immense — such as Canada or Australia — but whose total populations remain far smaller than Tokyo’s. Colors should probably be: 🟥 Countries with a 𝘀𝗺𝗮𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗿 population ⬜ Countries with a 𝗹𝗮𝗿𝗴𝗲𝗿 population 🟢 Tokyo metropolitan area Philip, Croyden, I think we can forgive The World in Maps this time 😉. results.snippet: As of 2026, the United Nations estimates the total population at 36,954,512. It is the second-largest single metropolitan area in the world in terms of built-up
